summary: Comprehensive test coverage audit completed for OSS Radar. Analyzed 14 test files covering 192 passing tests across all modules. Identified critical gaps: ladder.py (fallback logic) has zero test coverage (severity 5/5), email SMTP retry/sendmail fallback untested (4/5), scheduler cron validation missing (4/5). Found over-reliance on mocks vs integration tests, missing edge case coverage (empty inputs, Unicode, boundaries), and weak assertion patterns. Key modules audited: email/sender, scheduling/scheduler, ladder, models, scraping (Reddit, HTTP security), ranking (filters, scorer), pipeline, storage/database, LLM backend, synthetic generator. Provided specific test recommendations with severity ratings for each gap. Prioritized 10 critical missing tests including all fallback ladder logic, SMTP delivery ladder error handling, invalid cron expression validation, DNS rebinding/redirect SSRF checks, pipeline crash recovery, database corruption handling, Reddit OAuth failures, URL hash normalization, invalid weight validation, and sentiment boundary testing. Recommended short-term fixes (test_ladder.py, expand email tests, edge cases), medium-term integration tests using synthetic data, and long-term mutation/property-based testing. Current functional coverage estimated 65-70%, target 80%+ with full edge case and integration coverage. All 192 existing tests passing. Effort estimate 3-5 days for priority 1-5 items. Created TEST_COVERAGE_AUDIT.md with detailed findings, test suggestions, and roadmap.
